title: C Do-While Loop
content:
### Do-While Loop in C

The `do-while` loop is similar to `while`, but it guarantees the loop body executes at least once.

### Syntax

```c
do {
  // code to repeat
} while (condition);
```

### While vs Do-While

| Feature | `while` | `do-while` |
|---------|---------|------------|
| Condition check | At start | At end |
| Minimum executions | 0 | 1 |
| When to use | May not need to run | Must run at least once |
| Typical use | Counting loops | Menu validation |

### Common Use Cases

- Menu-driven programs
- Input validation (ask at least once)
- Game loops that process at least one frame
- State machines

---

> **Key Point:** Use `do-while` when the loop body must execute at least once regardless of the condition. Always end with `while (condition);` including the semicolon.

quiz.question: How many times does a do-while loop body always execute?
